general_hypothesis: Before any RayDPT work the BatVision U-Net reference must be re-anchored under the PLANAR depth target, and we must know which binaural input representation the reference actually needs: (a) how much do the derived interaural cues (ILD, cosIPD, sinIPD) add over the bare [L,R] magnitude pair; (b) does log1p magnitude compression help or hurt.
detailed_hypothesis: log1p compression equalises the dynamic range between the direct sound and the far-field echo tail, so it should help a model that must read weak late reflections to place distant surfaces -> log wins, and it should help the 2ch stack MORE than the 5ch stack (in 5ch, ILD already supplies a log-domain ratio, so part of the compression benefit is redundant). Adding ILD+IPD gives explicit interaural level/phase, which is where azimuth and hence per-direction depth structure lives -> 5ch beats 2ch at both log settings.
experiment_note: 2x2 grid on run_base.py, 1h wall-clock each, strictly sequential on one GPU. E0 batvision_2ch_nolog, E1 batvision_2ch_log, E2 batvision_5ch_nolog, E3 batvision_5ch_log. Driver utils/run_batvision_grid.sh. All four best-checkpoint selected on the composite.
scientific_conclusion: PASS, with the mechanism partially supported and the interaction REFUTED. (1) Derived interaural cues genuinely add information: +0.0209 (nolog) / +0.0217 (log) composite, both above sigma~0.008, and REPRODUCED at both log settings. Their effect is almost entirely on d1 (+0.0153/+0.0141) with rmse essentially unchanged (+0.0021/-0.0028): ILD and IPD encode AZIMUTH, so they improve angular assignment, not range. (2) log1p compression gives a small, consistent benefit: +0.0070 (2ch) / +0.0078 (5ch). Each draw alone sits at/below the noise floor, but the sign and magnitude reproduce across two independent channel counts, and the metric decomposition supports the stated mechanism: log acts on RMSE (-0.0070/-0.0119), i.e. RANGE, exactly as the dynamic-range argument predicts (compressing the direct sound makes weak far-field echoes readable). (3) The predicted INTERACTION is refuted: log did not help more at 2ch (0.0070) than at 5ch (0.0078). The two effects are ADDITIVE, not redundant. Scientifically the useful result is the dissociation: cues buy ANGLE, log buys RANGE. They fix different failure modes, which is why they compose. Reference champion = E3 (5ch log), composite 1.8567.
failure_mode: Predicted interaction absent: ILD's log-domain ratio does NOT make magnitude compression redundant. Also every cell peaked at epoch 13-14 of 25-26 and then overfit (val loss rising), so the 1h budget is partly spent making the model worse -- a confound for every future comparison (see D2, backlog training-optimization).
provenance: Runs E0-E3 on branch 2026-July-RayDPT-validation, commit 209c6e8, planar target (87b3047). Noise sigma~0.008 from the phase's prior reruns; single draw per cell, so the log effect is NOT crowned -- it is a consistent-sign near-noise effect awaiting a third confirming draw.

general_hypothesis: RayDPT must be re-anchored under the PLANAR target before any of its mechanisms can be judged; nothing before commit 87b3047 is comparable.
detailed_hypothesis: Run train.py unchanged for one wall-clock hour to produce the parent for all future RayDPT studies.
scientific_conclusion: ANCHOR, not a verdict. E4 fitted 5 epochs at 713 s/epoch versus batvision's 25 at ~130 s; its best checkpoint was the LAST epoch and every val metric was still improving. Its composite 2.0471 measures the wall-clock BUDGET, not the architecture. GPU profiling: cross-attention is 67% of forward. 68.7% of its parameters (e5..e8) were dead -- built, never called, no gradient.
failure_mode: Compute starvation under a wall-clock budget (D5). Reading the number as an architectural result would have been the error.

general_hypothesis: Depth from echoes is time-of-flight: if the input cannot resolve arrival time, no decoder can resolve distance. The STFT's temporal RESOLUTION is set by the analysis WINDOW, not the hop.
detailed_hypothesis: win=400 smears an echo over 1.417 m of one-way depth -- essentially the achieved RMSE. Arm A (win400 hop40) raises sampling density with the smear unchanged; arm B (win64 hop16) cuts the smear 6.2x. Only B should improve RMSE.
experiment_note: E5 arm A, E6 arm B, on run_base.py (cheapest parent). Control = E2.
scientific_conclusion: FAIL as a mechanism, though both arms improved the composite. The pre-registered prediction inverted: arm A -- which changed no resolution at all -- owns the RMSE gain (-0.0227), while arm B (6.2x finer smear) bought d1 (+0.0067) and left RMSE worse than A. By D3's rule a time-of-flight gain must appear in RANGE, so the mechanism is unsupported. Nyquist explains why arm A added ~no information (hop160 already samples the envelope at 1.2x Nyquist). E8 (bilinear resize, information held fixed) recovered 52% of arm A's RMSE gain but NO composite gain. E6 is confounded three ways: smear, staircase fineness, and frequency resolution all change at once.
failure_mode: Score moved while the mechanism story failed in both arms -- the case program.md warns to separate. E6 remains the batvision champion as a SCORE, not a mechanism.
provenance: commit b9c2f71. Composite monotone in staircase block width across E2/E5/E6, which is why I10 was run.

general_hypothesis: Under a wall-clock budget, compute spent per step must be repaid in epochs. A model that cannot converge is not being evaluated on its mechanism.
detailed_hypothesis: Cut the decode scale to 32x64 (I2's oracle: a PERFECT 32x64 predictor scores composite 0.3527 while RayDPT sits at 2.0471) and the cross-attention to one layer; keep ray-conditioning. Measured 147.3 s/epoch.
scientific_conclusion: PASS. E9 fitted 21 epochs and CONVERGED (best epoch 18 of 21, val loss flat over the last four), beating the starved E4 by 0.1162 -- about 14 sigma. D5 confirmed: RayDPT was compute-starved, not beaten. Two of my own LSA 'optimisations' were measured SLOWER than the original unfold and reverted; a CPU forward-time proxy had motivated both. The throughput bench under-predicted epochs (24.4 vs 21) because it timed train steps only, omitting ~14 s/epoch of validation.
failure_mode: None for the study. Methodological: benchmarks that omit validation overstate epochs-fit; CPU proxies do not predict GPU wall-clock.
provenance: commit bb9692d. COMPOUND change -- nothing attributable to a single part.

general_hypothesis: E9 is a compound change; before asking whether ray-conditioning helps, find which removed capacity was load-bearing.
detailed_hypothesis: The audio<->ray cross-attention is where per-ray direction information enters, so halving it is the change most likely to have cost the d1 deficit. Restore ray_cross_layers=2 at decode 32.
scientific_conclusion: INCONCLUSIVE. d1 moved only +0.0034 (12% of the gap, below sigma) but E10 never converged: 15 epochs at 232.3 s/epoch, best checkpoint = LAST epoch. Its d1 is a lower bound on a still-learning model, so the question is unanswered rather than answered negatively. What it DID show: 15 unconverged epochs at 2 layers beat 21 converged epochs at 1 layer (1.9192 vs 1.9308, above sigma). Capacity helps per epoch; the budget takes it back.
failure_mode: The same wall-clock confound that produced D2 and D5 blocked a third conclusion. The real constraint is the COST of a cross layer, not their number (I12).

general_hypothesis: D9 claimed RayDPT's entire deficit is angular (d1), indicting ray-conditioning where it claims to win. But no 2-layer RayDPT had ever converged, so d1 had never been read on a finished model.
detailed_hypothesis: The cost of a cross layer is its KV set, not its FFN: shrinking cr32's KV from e3 (2048 tokens) to e4 (512) is 4x cheaper at identical parameter count. That buys a converged 2-layer model.
experiment_note: E11 (L2, kv=e4) then E12 (L1, kv=e4) to complete the 2x2.
scientific_conclusion: PASS with reframing. E11 converged (23 epochs, best at 22) and d1 recovered +0.0079, closing 25% of the gap: D9 is partly a budget artefact and partly not. The 2x2 attributes it additively -- coarse KV +0.0033, second layer +0.0046 -- and neither alone clears sigma. Then a FULL-VAL spatial diagnostic overturned the framing entirely: the deficit is flat across azimuth (std 0.0056 on a mean of 0.0245), ZERO on the floor, and explodes with GT depth (8-9 m: batvision 0.4023 vs RayDPT 0.1751). Both models predict a GT 8.5 m surface at ~5 m. d1 is a +-25% RELATIVE threshold, so far-field compression is fatal to it while RMSE ignores it.
failure_mode: H1 (ray-conditioning does not aid angular assignment) and H4 (two receivers under-determine azimuth) both assumed an azimuthal story and are MIS-SPECIFIED. H6 and H7 were refuted at zero GPU cost. D10: small-sample diagnostics reversed the sign of both d1 and the spectrum -- three earlier conclusions were stated with unearned precision, though none was overturned on re-check.
provenance: commit c147692 / 1b995ab. All diagnostics now run on the full val set with an instrument check against the training logs.

general_hypothesis: A loss chooses what a model hedges toward. Masked MAE drives each pixel to the conditional median; where far depths are a minority of the mass, the median sits short.
detailed_hypothesis: Charge relative error instead -- |D-gt|/gt, or the symmetric |log D - log gt| -- so a 25% mistake costs the same at 1 m and 9 m, which is what d1 measures. Expect d1 to rise, driven by the 7-10 m deciles; RMSE to worsen slightly; ABS_REL to improve by construction and therefore not count.
experiment_note: E13 rel_mae, E14 log_mae, on E11's exact architecture.
scientific_conclusion: FAIL by the pre-registered falsification. Both arms made the 7-10 m deciles WORSE (rel_mae -0.19/-0.13/-0.22; log_mae -0.13/-0.00/-0.12) and overall d1 fell (0.5710 -> 0.5464 / 0.5538). The dense MAE is NOT the cause of far-field compression. My reasoning had a sign error: |D-gt|/gt divides by gt, making a far pixel's error CHEAPER, and it is asymmetric -- under-prediction is bounded at 1.0 while over-prediction is unbounded, so calling it short is always cheaper. The error budget moved to NEAR pixels: E13's 0-1 m d1 (0.4987) beats every model including batvision (0.2890), while its 9-10 m prediction collapses to 3.814 m. log_mae is symmetric in the ratio and still hurts, so RELATIVITY itself is wrong here, not the asymmetry.
failure_mode: Sign error in the mechanism, reproduced exactly in the data. ABS_REL improved by 0.1117 -- the one movement pre-registered as inadmissible.
provenance: commit d38ecc7. The DIAGNOSIS (far-field compression is where d1 is lost) is measured and survives the failed remedy; it motivated I14.
